title: fix: cohere brand logo + strip provider names from model descriptions
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
Post-merge cleanup for #11299.

**1. Cohere brand logo** — `cohere-embed-v4` landed with `brand: \"Cohere\"` but no matching logo file, so the dashboard model card fell back to no logo.
- New monochrome SVG at `enter.pollinations.ai/frontend/public/brand-logos/cohere.svg` (uses `currentColor` like the other 22 brand logos)
- Added `Cohere: \"cohere\"` entry to the `BRAND_LOGOS` map in [model-info.ts](enter.pollinations.ai/frontend/src/components/models/model-info.ts)

**2. Strip hosting-provider names from descriptions** — per [model-management skill §11.2](.claude/skills/model-management/SKILL.md#L562), descriptions must not carry provider/inference attribution (\"via Azure\", \"via DashScope\", etc.). The \`provider\` and \`brand\` fields already carry that.
- 2 of mine from #11299 (\`cohere-embed-v4\`, \`qwen3-embedding-8b\`)
- 5 pre-existing drift across \`text.ts\`, \`image.ts\` (Wan 2.6/2.2/2.7, Qwen Image Plus, Qwen3 Coder Next, Seedance 2.0)

gist.headline: A fresh coat of polish
gist.blurb: We've added the official Cohere logo to our dashboard and cleaned up some model descriptions for a sleeker look. Just a little housekeeping to keep the hive looking sharp!
gist.summary: Added the Cohere brand logo to the dashboard and removed redundant hosting-provider names from seven model descriptions across the text, image, and embedding registries.
gist.impact: Model cards on the dashboard now display the correct Cohere branding and feature cleaner descriptions without repetitive provider tags.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee wearing a painter's beret, carefully polishing a Cohere logo on a dashboard screen, assisted by the monitor robot holding cleaning supplies.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny painter's beret, carefully polishing a glowing Cohere logo on a retro computer monitor. A cute chunky monitor robot stands nearby holding a small pixelated cleaning cloth. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
